text: The surgeon mobilizes the ascending colon and performs a Kocher maneuver on the duodenum to expose the inferior vena cava (IVC). The lower pole of the kidney is mobilized, and the gonadal vein is identified and ligated, then cut to improve access around the arteriovenous malformation (AVM) sac.

The surgeon performs dissection along the lateral border of the inferior vena cava (IVC) to access the renal hilum. The anterior division of the right renal artery is identified first, followed by the posterior division, which runs alongside a branch of the renal vein. The main trunk of the right renal artery is visualized during this process.

The surgeon loops the right renal vein and the anterior division of the right renal artery, followed by the main trunk of the right renal artery to achieve full vascular control. Tightening the loop around the main trunk causes the sac to deflate, which refills upon relaxation. Similarly, tightening the loop around the anterior division results in sac deflation. The sac is dissected to reveal the ostium on the anterior division of the renal artery, as indicated by preoperative angiography. Two small aneurysms are visible near the ostium site.

A vascular clamp is applied to the anterior division of the renal artery for vascular control. The single arterial feeder to the arteriovenous malformation (AVM) is identified, clipped, and cut. The margins of the ostium are freshened and repaired using a Gore-Tex suture. After the ostium is closed, the vascular clamp is removed to assess hemostasis.

The surgeon dissects the sac from the surrounding parenchyma tissue, which begins to bleed. A vascular clamp is reapplied to the main arterial trunk. The sac is observed draining into a branch of the renal vein, which is then ligated and cut. The vascular clamp is removed, irrigation is performed, and hemostasis is secured. Renorraphy is completed using 3-0 V-Loc sutures in two layers.

The surgeon reapplies vascular clamps proximally and distally to reinforce the small aneurysms near the repaired ostea using a pledget sutured with Gore-Tex suture. After removing the clamps and slings, hemostasis is checked, and flow seal is applied to the parenchyma region. A layer of fat is sutured over the kidney.
